name: Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop
name_original: Castillo y Embalse de Nogales — circular desde Nogales
description: Hike the moderate 9.2-mile circular route around Nogales, exploring the historic Castillo de Nogales and scenic Embalse de Nogales.
region_intro: Hiking around Nogales offers trails through varied terrain, including wooded hills, agricultural lands, and areas around historical castles and reservoirs. The region features a mix of moderate climbs and gentler paths, providing diverse experiences for hikers. These routes often traverse open countryside and offer views of the local landscape. Best hiking trails around Nogales The most popular hiking route is Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route features moderate elevation gain through the countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194), a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail explores a limestone mountain range with scenic views. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through historical areas and local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around Nogales is defined by wooded hills, agricultural paths, and historical landmarks like castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Nogales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 55 hikers have used komoot to explore Nogales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Nogales?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Nogales, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging moderate and difficult treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Nogales?
A: Hiking around Nogales offers a unique blend of landscapes. You'll find trails traversing wooded hills, rolling grasslands, and the distinctive 'sky islands' where mountains rise dramatically. The region also features routes through agricultural lands, along creeks, and near historical landmarks, providing varied scenery.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Nogales?
A: Yes, Nogales offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. The guide lists 4 easy routes, and the region generally provides options for gentle paths through the countryside. These trails are ideal for enjoying the natural beauty without strenuous effort.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Nogales area?
A: Yes, many routes around Nogales are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219153536/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-salvaleon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991603066/limestone-mountain-range-circular-from-la-parra-sl-ba-194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991621788/castillo-de-nogales-circular-by-los-ejidos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while hiking?
A: The Nogales area is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991621788/castillo-de-nogales-circular-by-los-ejidos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos&lt;/a&gt; route. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Battle of Albuera Memorial Mosaic&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by reservoirs or water features?
A: Yes, you can find trails that include water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222605650/nogales-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past a reservoir. Additionally, the broader region features areas like Patagonia Lake State Park and Sonoita Creek State Natural Area, known for their scenic creek trails and opportunities for birdwatching along the water.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Nogales?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Nogales, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, which includes wooded hills and agricultural paths, as well as the historical landmarks like castles that add a unique dimension to the routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Can I find moderate to challenging hikes in Nogales?
A: Absolutely. The majority of trails in Nogales are rated as moderate, with 15 such routes available. These include paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991605926/ride-to-trasierra-circular-from-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to Trasierra - circular from La Parra&lt;/a&gt;, which features moderate elevation gain through the countryside. There are also 2 difficult routes for those seeking a more strenuous adventure.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nogales?
A: The Nogales region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, the unique 'sky island' topography means that even in warmer months, higher elevations can offer cooler temperatures. Generally, spring and fall provide ideal weather for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while hiking in Nogales?
A: Yes, the Nogales area is excellent for birdwatching, especially along its water features. The Sonoita Creek State Natural Area, with its year-round springs and creeks, provides distinctive habitats for migrating birds and hummingbirds. The Juan Bautista de Anza National Historic Trail, particularly the section from Tumacácori to Tubac along the Santa Cruz River, also offers peaceful walks and prime birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Are there any trails that offer scenic viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Given the 'sky island' topography where mountains rise dramatically from the valley floors, many trails around Nogales offer scenic views. Routes exploring the Limestone Mountain Range, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991606066/limestone-mountain-range-circular-from-la-parra-sl-ba-194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194)&lt;/a&gt;, provide picturesque and varied scenery. The region's wooded hills and waving grasslands also contribute to beautiful vistas.

Q: Is it possible to combine hiking with exploring nearby towns or cultural sites?
A: Yes, the Nogales area allows for a rich combination of hiking and cultural exploration. Trails often pass through or near historical areas and settlements. For instance, the Juan Bautista de Anza National Historic Trail connects Tumacácori National Historical Park with Tubac, offering both scenic hiking and a glimpse into the region's past. You can also visit nearby towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corte de Peleas&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Located in the Badajoz town of Nogales, this medieval fortress built in the 15th century sits on a small hill and its character is purely defensive. For this reason, its most outstanding element is the keep surrounded by a wall, since it was of great importance during the clashes with the French and the Portuguese.
Picturesque path next to the Nogales reservoir, which also passes through the dam, built in 1991.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Castillo y Embalse de Nogales circular trail?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate hiking trail. It requires a good level of physical fitness due to its length of nearly 15 kilometers and an elevation gain of around 210 meters. It's best suited for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this route is approximately 4 hours, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the sights.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily accessible paths, often referred to as 'sendas de fácil acceso'. You'll traverse areas around the sierra and pass through a river area ('rivera'). Be aware that during winter, the river area can carry significant water, potentially making it slippery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter two significant highlights: the historic Nogales Castle, a 15th-century fortress offering panoramic views, and the picturesque Nogales Reservoir, which provides beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, consider visiting during spring or autumn for more comfortable temperatures. If hiking in winter, be prepared for potentially slippery conditions near the river area due to increased water levels.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo y Embalse de Nogales trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, especially around wildlife and other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Castillo y Embalse de Nogales circular hike?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts and ends in the town of Nogales. You can find parking options within the town itself, making it easy to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available near the trailhead or along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in Nogales, where you can find basic services such as cafes and bars. However, there are no specific facilities or water sources directly on the trail itself, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy wonderful panoramic views of the interior landscape of Badajoz, including the Dehesa landscape. The route is rich in natural beauty, featuring extensions of oleanders, brooms, and holm oaks, alongside the impressive Nogales Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for children or those with limited mobility?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, length, and elevation gain, this trail is generally not recommended for young children or individuals with limited mobility. It requires a good level of physical fitness.
